title: rosa sempervirens subsp. arvensis
intro: Rosa sempervirens subsp. arvensis, also known as the wild rose or field rose, is a subspecies of the evergreen rose (Rosa sempervirens) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant's hips, which are the fruit of the rose, are rich in vitamin C and are often used to make jams, preserves, and other culinary products.
